title: Racosperma blayanum
taxonomy: Racosperma blayanum is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Australia and was previously classified under the genus Acacia. However, the genus Acacia was split into several genera in 2001, and Racosperma blayanum was reclassified accordingly. This reclassification was part of a broader effort to refine the classification of the Acacia family. The species is now recognized as a distinct taxon within the Racosperma genus.

intro: Racosperma blayanum, also known as Blayney wattle or Blayney black wattle, is a species of shrub or small tree in the family Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Racosperma blayanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
